zotero.abstractNote: Simulation-based inference (SBI) is a set of methods for performing Bayesian inference when the likelihood function is intractable but the model can be simulated. sbi is a Python package that provides a comprehensive, modular, and easy-to-use interface for modern SBI algorithms, including neural posterior estimation, neural likelihood estimation, and neural ratio estimation. It is designed to be flexible, allowing users to easily swap between different inference algorithms and models, and to be integrated into existing scientific workflows.
